rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are explicitly declared in the AADL model.
Constants and equations in AGREE contracts must be declared with matching AADL data types.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or AADL properties that are not present in the analyzed model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for structural assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume numeric bounds or types unless explicitly stated in the requirement and compatible with AADL declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of component structure
